activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, removing the incorrect «to» after the modal.
activities.prompt: Nora can to use the design software confidently.

evaluator.answer: Nora can use the design software confidently

activities.explanation: Después de «can» usamos directamente el infinitivo «use».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the negative modal form.
activities.prompt: I don't can hear you clearly.

evaluator.answer: I can't hear you clearly

activities.explanation: La negativa de «can» es «can't», sin auxiliar «do».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, removing the unnecessary past auxiliary.
activities.prompt: When Leo was six, he did could count to a hundred.

evaluator.answer: When Leo was six, he could count to a hundred

activities.explanation: «Could» ya marca el pasado y no se combina con «did».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the sentence with «be able to» to emphasize one completed success.
activities.prompt: The door was jammed, but we could open it after three attempts.

evaluator.answer: The door was jammed, but we were able to open it after three attempts

activities.explanation: La norma editorial usa «were able to» para un logro puntual afirmativo.

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, removing the incorrect «to».
activities.prompt: We couldn't to access the account with the backup code.

evaluator.answer: We couldn't access the account with the backup code

activities.explanation: Después de «couldn't» usamos directamente «access».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the future ability form.
activities.prompt: After the course, you will can edit videos independently.

evaluator.answer: After the course, you will be able to edit videos independently

activities.explanation: `Can` no tiene futuro propio; usamos «will be able to».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the present perfect ability form.
activities.prompt: I haven't can contact the supplier this week.

evaluator.answer: I haven't been able to contact the supplier this week

activities.explanation: El present perfect requiere «haven't been able to».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the infinitive of ability.
activities.prompt: Mina wants to can drive before she moves abroad.

evaluator.answer: Mina wants to be able to drive before she moves abroad

activities.explanation: `Can` no tiene infinitivo; usamos «to be able to».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete informal permission request.
activities.prompt: Do I can borrow your charger?

evaluator.answer: Can I borrow your charger

activities.explanation: El modal ocupa la primera posición: «Can I borrow ...?».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete courteous permission request.
activities.prompt: Could I to leave a little early today?

evaluator.answer: Could I leave a little early today

activities.explanation: Después de «Could I» usamos el infinitivo sin «to».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete formal permission request.
activities.prompt: May I to see your identification, please?

evaluator.answer: May I see your identification, please

activities.explanation: Después de «may» usamos directamente «see».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the passive permission form.
activities.prompt: Only laboratory staff are allow to enter the storage room.

evaluator.answer: Only laboratory staff are allowed to enter the storage room

activities.explanation: La pasiva requiere el participio «allowed».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, removing the incorrect «to» after «could».
activities.prompt: From the balcony, we could to see the whole harbour.

evaluator.answer: From the balcony, we could see the whole harbour

activities.explanation: La percepción usa «could see», sin «to».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the «be able to» construction.
activities.prompt: After the explanation, I was able understand the problem.

evaluator.answer: After the explanation, I was able to understand the problem

activities.explanation: La construcción completa es «was able to understand».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, removing third-person «-s» from the modal.
activities.prompt: Ravi cans repair most bicycle brakes.

evaluator.answer: Ravi can repair most bicycle brakes

activities.explanation: Los modales no añaden `-s`: «Ravi can repair».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete question, removing the unnecessary auxiliary.
activities.prompt: Did Lena could swim when she was five?

evaluator.answer: Could Lena swim when she was five

activities.explanation: La pregunta modal comienza con «Could Lena».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the verb after «can».
activities.prompt: Sam can speaks Arabic and French.

evaluator.answer: Sam can speak Arabic and French

activities.explanation: Después del modal usamos el infinitivo «speak».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, removing the incorrect «to».
activities.prompt: With the new keyboard, I can to type much faster.

evaluator.answer: With the new keyboard, I can type much faster

activities.explanation: La forma correcta es «can type».

activities.instructions: Correct the short response so it grants immediate permission naturally.
activities.prompt: Reply to “Could I open the window?”: Yes, you could.

evaluator.answer: Yes, you can

activities.explanation: El permiso inmediato se concede normalmente con «can».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the rule-based prohibition.
activities.prompt: Contractors don't allowed to enter the server room alone.

evaluator.answer: Contractors aren't allowed to enter the server room alone

activities.explanation: La pasiva negativa usa «aren't allowed to».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the construction after «managed».
activities.prompt: After three attempts, the rescue team managed lifting the fallen beam.

evaluator.answer: After three attempts, the rescue team managed to lift the fallen beam

activities.explanation: La construcción es «managed to + infinitivo».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the failed-attempt form.
activities.prompt: Despite two restarts, we didn't could restore the connection.

evaluator.answer: Despite two restarts, we couldn't restore the connection

activities.explanation: La negativa pasada del modal es «couldn't».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting agreement in the past permission form.
activities.prompt: During the renovation, the employees was allowed to work from the library.

evaluator.answer: During the renovation, the employees were allowed to work from the library

activities.explanation: El sujeto plural «the employees» exige «were allowed to».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the future permission form.
activities.prompt: After induction, volunteers will allowed to use the workshop.

evaluator.answer: After induction, volunteers will be allowed to use the workshop

activities.explanation: La pasiva futura requiere «will be allowed to».

activities.instructions: Rewrite both sentences, correcting future ability and formal permission.
activities.prompt: After the update, users will can export reports. May I to test the feature now?

evaluator.answer: After the update, users will be able to export reports. May I test the feature now

activities.explanation: Usamos «will be able to» y «May I + infinitivo».
